during_correctness_wins.name: locale_root_lowercase
during_correctness_wins.reason: DuckLake canonical column-type names ('list', 'struct', 'map', 'int32', 'decimal(p,s)', etc.) are ASCII. Default-locale lowercase folds Turkish 'I'/'İ' incorrectly and would silently fall through every when-branch. Pre-existing as TODO(kit: kit-locale) per PLAN.md DURING.
during_correctness_wins.helper: inlined — single call site; java.util.Locale was already imported

during_correctness_wins.name: safe_sql_message
during_correctness_wins.reason: All 9 sites already used null-safe access patterns: six pass `e.message` directly into System.Logger.log() (accepts Any?, no NPE possible); two declared the message as a `val` of nullable type and explicitly null-tested it (`?: return false` and `message != null && (…)`). The trino-main precedent for kit-sql-msg was `e.message!!` → `e.message ?: "(no message)"`; no such unsafe sites exist in catalog-main. Markers planted defensively by the port pass; verified each before removing.

idiom_cleanup.name: kotlin_collection_factories
idiom_cleanup.reason: Kotlin built-ins are equivalent for defensive copies of non-null collections; reads more naturally and drops the java.util.* qualifier noise. None of the sites were `java.util.List.of(arg, arg, …)` with explicit arguments (the case that subtly changes the returned type's identity).
files: WriteChange.kt (2 sites — InsertedIntoTable / DeletedFromTable secondary ctors)
idiom_cleanup.kept_at: no java.util.List.of(arg) with explicit arguments existed in catalog-main

idiom_cleanup.name: drop_requireNonNull_on_kotlin_non_null_params
idiom_cleanup.before: this.x = requireNonNull(x, "x is null") on Kotlin non-null `String` constructor params
idiom_cleanup.reason: Kotlin compiler emits Intrinsics.checkNotNullParameter for non-nullable params; requireNonNull is dead code on those paths.
kept_at: SnapshotRange.kt:42-43 — `begin`, `end` are Field<Long>? (true null assertion when jOOQ Table.field() can't resolve the column by name)

idiom_cleanup.name: promote_jackson_data_class_to_jvm_record
idiom_cleanup.reason: Matches the @JvmRecord style established by the other 6 Jackson handles in this package (DucklakeWriteFragment, DucklakePartitionSpec, DucklakeFileColumnStats, DucklakeDeleteFragment, DucklakeColumn, DucklakePartitionField). No secondary constructor, no init block, so the @JvmRecord constraint set is satisfied.
not_promoted: DucklakeNameMapEntry.kt — has two secondary constructors (constructor(sourceName, targetFieldId) and constructor(sourceName, targetFieldId, children)); @JvmRecord forbids secondary constructors so it stays plain `data class`.

not_applied.name: collapse_optional_to_nullable_on_internal_paths
not_applied.rationale: Same SPI/wire-boundary risk as documented in trino-main kit-notes. Catalog has many Optional<T> fields on Jackson-serialized handles (e.g. DucklakeWriteFragment.partitionId: OptionalLong, DucklakeWriteFragment.nameMap: Optional<DucklakeNameMap>); flipping these to T? changes the JSON property layout and breaks wire-format compatibility. Splitting internal-only Optional sites from wire/SPI sites requires per-call-site analysis with poor risk/value.

not_applied.name: drop_jvmstatic_on_companion_helpers
not_applied.rationale: Unlike trino-main (now 100% Kotlin in scope), the catalog module ships alongside `jvm/ducklake-catalog/generated/` (jOOQ codegen — Java, stays Java forever) and is consumed by downstream modules that include Java code paths (trino-ducklake currently 100% Kotlin, but future plugins / extensions may not be). The brief explicitly directs us to KEEP @JvmStatic everywhere it currently exists for this slice. Inspection confirms 16 .kt files use @JvmStatic; none were touched.
not_applied.decision: Deliberately kept by policy. See brief §'Mandatory rule changes from trino-main'.

not_applied.name: resource_leak_fixes
not_applied.rationale: PLAN.md DURING lists DuckDbFileWriter / DucklakeMergeSink / DucklakeMetadata — all live in trino-ducklake, not in catalog. `grep finally` across jvm/ducklake-catalog/src/**/*.kt returned zero matches; nothing to fix here.

not_applied.name: getRetainedSizeInBytes_double_count
not_applied.rationale: Catalog module has no value/handle types that expose a `getRetainedSizeInBytes()` (this is a Trino airlift-stats SPI pattern, used by DucklakeSplit / DucklakeColumnHandle in trino-ducklake). No call sites in catalog.

shared_helpers_decision.choice: inline — no IdiomKit.kt in this slice
shared_helpers_decision.rationale: The trino-main precedent introduced IdiomKit.lowercaseRoot / safeMessage / firstLine / firstLineOf but noted in its own kit-notes that the helpers were 'introduced, not yet rolled out' because each DURING fix was a one-liner. catalog-main has exactly one locale fix and zero actual SQL-message fixes (the 9 markers were defensively planted and the underlying code was already safe). With <2 real call sites, a dedicated helper file would add more noise than it removes. If a future catalog change does need the helper, this is the kit-notes entry to update; the trino-main IdiomKit is available as the canonical reference.

verification.expected: ducklake-catalog 108/0/0/0 across 33 suites; trino-ducklake 825/0/0/0 across 70 suites
verification.last_run: 2026-06-01 — both modules passed. Parsed from on-disk TEST-*.xml: ducklake-catalog tests=108 failures=0 errors=0 skipped=0 (33 testsuites); trino-ducklake tests=825 failures=0 errors=0 skipped=0 (70 testsuites).
